params.name: Zeus Statue at Olympia
params.description:
On Dec 21, 426 AD, Rome issued an edict to destroy pagan temples, and the statue of Zeus was transported to Constantinople.
The Temple of Zeus was built for the Greek deity Zeus and is located in the southeast of the Acropolis of Athens, which is considered to be under the control of the king of gods Zeus. The central part of the temple is occupied by the statue of Zeus, which was created by the sculptor Phidias. The statue is about 12 meters high, wearing a golden crown, sitting on a magnifi …
